observed_text_zh: 抱歉，系统超时，请稍后重试。
observed_text_translation: Sorry, system timeout, please retry later.
notes: Iter8 first REAL id=53 deployment with HTTP-layer fast-completion (Connection: keep-alive, X-Response-Complete:1, Content-Length, single 'finished' status event). Frontend STILL renders native 系统超时 — same surface verdict as #991 (iter6) and #994 (iter7). HOWEVER significant behavioral delta: response arrived in ~10-15s (fast) vs iter6/iter7 which hung the full timeout window before the same message appeared. This strongly suggests: (a) the iter8 envelope IS being delivered to the frontend (engine intercept fired on id=53), (b) the wall-clock fast-completion DID work, but (c) the qianwen frontend's success criteria for the SSE body is stricter than 'connection closes with finished status' — it likely requires actual content chunks before any finished marker, otherwise it classifies the conversation as a system timeout. Next axis suggestion: emit a non-empty content chunk (the warning text body) BEFORE the finished status event, mimicking real qianwen_sse content delivery sequence rather than a pure status-only envelope.
